title: Ledger HTTP service (4-stage, compounding)
run_contract: The produced project MUST include an executable `start.sh` at the repo root that launches the service IN THE FOREGROUND on the port given by the PORT env var (PORT=0 means pick any free port), binds 127.0.0.1, and prints exactly `LISTENING <port>` to stdout once bound. The runner detects readiness and the assigned port from that line. The runner talks to the project ONLY over HTTP/JSON — no source is read. The agent (any arm) is shown this contract and the per-stage prose; it is NEVER shown the conformance suites or the invariant check.

named_invariant.statement: Transfers conserve the sum of all account balances (no money is created or destroyed) and no account balance is ever negative. A transfer that would violate either rule must fail atomically, leaving both accounts unchanged.

notes: Stages compound: S3 (withdraw) mirrors S2 (deposit) and rewards a good S2 abstraction; S4 (transfer) is the error-prone change that stresses the invariant via atomicity + non-negativity. Conformance suites are CUMULATIVE — each stage's suite includes all prior stages' tests, so a later stage breaking an earlier contract surfaces as a cross-stage regression.
